title: libpod: drop arbitrary memory limit of 4M
body:
drop the arbitrary limit of 4M for creating a container.

4M are not enough for runc to run, on Fedora it seems to require at least 7M, and it is too much for crun that requires less than 1 Mb.

Signed-off-by: Giuseppe Scrivano <gscrivan@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *giuseppe* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [giuseppe] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Hm. My only concern would be that, from what I remember, the `runc` "I don't have enough memory" errors are horribly unintuitive - you'd never know that the memory limit was too low. Should we consider adding a warning for limits of, say, 5mb and under, that the OCI runtime may not have enough memory to finish completing the container? That would be really annoying, but otherwise people will be very confused about why things aren't working.

> Hm. My only concern would be that, from what I remember, the `runc` "I don't have enough memory" errors are horribly unintuitive - you'd never know that the memory limit was too low

one issue is that the 4Mb limit doesn't help much with runc, as it requires at least 7Mb now.

We could make it a logrus.Warnf statement.  Then it would not show unless the user stated --log-level.
Also should we special case crun, to not need the message?
I this configurable from libpod.conf?  If not, I think it should be.

personally I don't think we should try to prevent runtime failures from Podman.  It should be the OCI runtime itself to show a better error message.

Another example:

```
# podman --runtime /usr/bin/runc run --rm  --pids-limit 1 alpine echo hi
Error: container_linux.go:346: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:319: getting the final child's pid from pipe caused \"EOF\"": OCI runtime error
```

Fair enough. LGTM then.

On Wed, Dec 18, 2019, 08:23 Giuseppe Scrivano <notifications@github.com>
